'''Greg Bird''' (born 10 February 1984) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played as a {{rlp|sr|lk}} or {{rlp|fe}} for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at international level.

Bird previously played for the [[NRL]] for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]]. He played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] in two separate spells, having previously played for the French club in 2009. He is a [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|NSW Country]], [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]], [[Prime Minister's XIII]] and [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] representative.

==Background==
Bird was born in [[Maitland, New South Wales]], [[Australia]].

He played junior football for the West Maitland Red Dogs and Maitland Pumpkin Pickers before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]]. He played for the Knights' [[Harold Matthews]] and [[SG Ball]] teams before being signed by the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]]. In 2001, while playing for Rutherford Technology High School, Bird was selected for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] and captained the side.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://maitland.yourguide.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/city-league-stars-aussie-jersey/680099.aspx|title=City league star's Aussie jersey|access-date=26 August 2008|publisher=Maitland Mercury|date=17 July 2001|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120722025522/http://maitland.yourguide.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/city-league-stars-aussie-jersey/680099.aspx|archive-date=22 July 2012}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=10 October 2008|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Professional playing career==
===Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks===
In round 7 of the [[2002 NRL season]], Bird made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/|title=Rleague.com - Houston, Chris|access-date=2012-08-01|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120618105021/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/h/houston_chris/index.php|archive-date=18 June 2012}}/db/player/b/bird_greg/index.php</ref><ref name="news">{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/sport/nrl/player/0,26796,100235-126,00.html |title=Player Profile |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007002055/http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/sport/nrl/player/0%2C26796%2C100235-126%2C00.html |archive-date=7 October 2008 |url-status=dead}}</ref> In 2003, he became a regular first-grader for the Sharks.

In 2004, Bird obtained notoriety after kneeing [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] {{rlp|wg}} [[Shane Marteene]] in the head during their round 4 clash, which resulted in him being sent off by the [[referee]] and being suspended from playing for 10 weeks.<ref name= "Sharks lash Bird suspension">{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/sport/content/s1083587.htm|title=ABC Sport – Rugby League – Sharks lash Bird suspension<!-- Bot generated title -->|website=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation]] |access-date=5 July 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20041112031955/http://www.abc.net.au/sport/content/s1083587.htm|archive-date=12 November 2004|url-status=dead}}</ref> According to his agent, Gavin Orr, Bird had "said he copped a couple of blows to the face during the match and he got an elbow from Shane Marteene in the tackle and just lost it."<ref name= "Bird says sorry">{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2004/04/04/1081017039232.html?from=storyrhs|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Bird says sorry for 'cowardly act' but he's prepared to pay the price|date=5 April 2004}}</ref> Chief executive of the Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks, [[Steve Rogers (rugby league)|Steve Rogers]], claimed that Bird had "been made an example of" and was angered that there "wasn't much consideration given to Greg's youth and his previous record".<ref name= "Sharks lash Bird suspension"/> The judiciary stated that it took Bird's remorse and good record into account, but decided upon the lengthy suspension due to the severity of the offence.<ref name= "Bird gets 10 weeks for kneeing">{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/sport/content/s1083470.htm|title=ABC Sport – Rugby League – Bird gets 10 weeks for kneeing<!-- Bot generated title -->|website=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation]] }}</ref> After the game Bird apologised to Marteene in the dressing rooms and also wrote letters to the player and the South Sydney Rabbitohs club.<ref name= "Sharks lash Bird suspension"/>

In 2006, Bird posed for the [[Naked Rugby League 2007-08]] charity calendar, which was produced to raise money for the National [[Breast Cancer]] Foundation of Australia.

In March 2006, Bird was alleged to have sent model [[Lara Bingle]] inappropriate text messages, although he denied this. Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks spokesman Rob Willis said "Greg's thrown up his hands. He says he's done nothing inappropriate. There's been nothing obscene written. There's been no harassment."<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/australias--face-claims-shark-attack/2006/03/03/1141191849454.html|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Crude texts less than model behaviour|date=4 March 2006}}</ref>

In 2006, Bird suffered a serious rib injury in round 21, against the [[Newcastle Knights]], which ended his season. He was voted as the Sharks' player of the year, despite missing 7 games of the season through injury.<ref>[http://www.sharks.com.au/index.php?id=79 Greg Bird<!-- Bot generated title -->] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20050317052911/http://www.sharks.com.au/index.php?id=79 |date=17 March 2005 }}</ref>

In May 2007, Bird was selected for the [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|New South Wales Country Origin]] team in the annual [[City vs Country Origin]] match. In June 2007, Bird was selected for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] side to play [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] in game 2 of the [[2007 State of Origin series]]. His performance earned him a recall for game 3, this time at five-eighth. He was awarded the man of the match award. In September 2007, Bird was selected for the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] team to play [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]]. In October 2007, Bird was selected for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] to play [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the one-off test match in [[Wellington, New Zealand]]. He scored a try on debut and was named the player's player.

In May 2008, Bird was selected for Australia in 2008 centenary test, retaining his five-eighth jersey in place of the injured [[Darren Lockyer]]. Bird was also selected for game 1 of the [[2008 State of Origin series]] for New South Wales, controversially chosen again in the five-eighth position. He was named man of the match and was again selected in game 2. In August 2008, Bird was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team#Rugby League World Cup Squad|preliminary 46-man Kangaroos squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|author=Liam FitzGibbon |publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia) |title=Surprises in Kangaroos squad |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24111780-23214,00.html |access-date=2 August 2008 |date=1 August 2008 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080808144730/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0%2C8659%2C24111780-23214%2C00.html |archive-date=8 August 2008}}</ref><ref name="Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.iht.com/articles/ap/2008/08/01/sports/AS-RGL-Australia-Squad.php|title=Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad|work=International Herald Tribune|date=1 August 2008|access-date=2 August 2008}}</ref> However, he didn't play a game in the tournament.

On 25 August 2008, Bird appeared in the Sutherland Local Court charged with maliciously inflicting grievous bodily harm on his 24-year-old American girlfriend, Katie Milligan, at his flat in [[Cronulla, New South Wales|Cronulla]]. It was alleged that he had smashed a glass into her face. It was reported that she underwent surgery for a fractured eye socket and facial injuries. Bird was granted bail. The Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks chief executive, Tony Zappia, advised Bird that he would be stood down from the playing roster pending further investigation. Milligan had moved to Sydney to be with Bird earlier in the month, having met him in Las Vegas while he was on holiday there earlier in the year.<ref name="Greg Bird charged with violent attack">{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,24237719-5001021,00.html|title=Greg Bird allegedly blames Brent Watson for glassing Katie Milligan|last=Gee|first=Steve|date=25 August 2008|work=The Daily Telegraph|access-date=25 August 2008}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|first=Stuart|last=Honeysett|url=http://www.theaustralian.news.com.au/story/0,25197,24241381-2722,00.html|title=Sharks' shame as Greg Bird bailed|access-date=25 August 2008|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=25 August 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080902001405/http://www.theaustralian.news.com.au/story/0,25197,24241381-2722,00.html|archive-date=2 September 2008|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/entertainment/story/0,26278,24165659-5013560,00|title=Katie Milligan flies into arms of Cronulla Sharks' Greg Bird|access-date=25 August 2008|work=news.com.au|publisher=News Ltd|date=25 August 2008}}</ref> In September 2008, Bird's legal representative was advised that Bird would be facing additional charges of public mischief and false accusation.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/greg-bird-new-charges/2008/09/29/1222650970757.html|title=Greg Bird faces mischief charge|last=Ramachandran|first=Arjun|date=29 September 2008|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|access-date=29 September 2008|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081002010552/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/greg-bird-new-charges/2008/09/29/1222650970757.html|archive-date=2 October 2008 }}</ref> The charges against Bird led to the British Government denying him a [[Work permit (United Kingdom)|work permit]] to take up a playing position with [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref name="Irvine"/>

On 17 January 2009, Bird signed a 1-year contract with the Bradford Bulls starting in the same year<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.sundayherald.com/sport/national_sport/index.var.217897.0.bulls_confirm_bird_signing.php|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120910180043/http://www.sundayherald.com/sport/national_sport/index.var.217897.0.bulls_confirm_bird_signing.php|url-status=dead|archive-date=10 September 2012|title=Bulls confirm Bird signing|date=17 January 2009|access-date=18 January 2009 }}</ref> after being released from his contract with the Sharks the day before.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/bird-no-longer-a-shark/2009/01/16/1231608974624.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090122230610/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/bird-no-longer-a-shark/2009/01/16/1231608974624.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=22 January 2009|title=Bird no longer a Shark|date=16 January 2009|access-date=16 January 2009}}</ref> However, he was unable to play for the Bulls as his visa was denied.<ref name="Irvine">{{cite news|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article5727891.ece|title=Bradford Bull suffer as Greg Bird is refused visa|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|date=14 February 2009|work=[[timesonline.co.uk]]|access-date=13 March 2009|location=London}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>
After initially deciding to play [[rugby union]] for the [[Southern Districts Rugby Club]] in the [[Shute Shield]], Bird eventually signed with French club the [[Catalans Dragons]] of the [[Super League]].<ref>[http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/bird-offered-french-lifeline/2009/03/04/1235842456881.html Bird offered French lifeline] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090305073818/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/bird-offered-french-lifeline/2009/03/04/1235842456881.html |date=5 March 2009 }}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 13 March 2009, Bird again appeared in the Sutherland Local Court on 5 charges; 1 charge with 4 backup charges, of common assault against another woman at Cronulla on 19 January 2008.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.lawlink.nsw.gov.au/courtlists/nswlc_lists.nsf/2d69be7802e95b764a256e6a00044f71/3bef15b1d7586e3dca25757300736faf?OpenDocument|title=SUTHERLAND Criminal Lists 13/03/2009|date=13 March 2009|work=lawlink.nsw.gov.au|access-date=13 March 2009}} {{Dead link|date=September 2010|bot=H3llBot}}</ref> This matter was not related to the case involving his girlfriend.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/articles/2009/03/13/1236447449356.html|title=Greg Bird accused of nightclub assault|last=Kontominas|first=Bellinda|date=13 March 2009|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|access-date=13 March 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090314013256/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/articles/2009/03/13/1236447449356.html|archive-date=14 March 2009 }}</ref> His accuser was found to have been the aggressor in the incident and Bird was cleared of all charges by the magistrate.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/greg-bird-cleared-of-nightclub-assault/story-e6frep5x-1225827110430|title=Greg Bird cleared of nightclub assault|date=5 February 2010|work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|access-date=23 June 2011}}</ref> On 28 April 2009, he was found guilty of a count of reckless wounding of his girlfriend and another count of making a false accusation to police after attempting to blame his flatmate.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/catalan/8022208.stm|title=Bird convicted over glass attack|date=28 April 2009|work=BBC News|access-date=28 April 2009}}</ref> On 6 May 2009, the Dragons opened talks with Bird about extending his contract with the club despite his court case on 28 April 2009 for assault.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/catalan/8035474.stm|title=Catalans to keep convicted Bird|date=6 May 2009|work=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=6 May 2009}}</ref> On 22 June 2009, he was sentenced to a maximum of 16 months imprisonment.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/leaguehq/articles/2009/06/22/1245522769014.html|title=Greg Bird jailed for 16 months|date=22 June 2009|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|access-date=22 June 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625002113/http://www.smh.com.au/leaguehq/articles/2009/06/22/1245522769014.html|archive-date=25 June 2009 }}</ref>

On appeal in November 2009, Bird was acquitted of the charges, after both he and his girlfriend gave evidence to support his claims.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/bird-free-as-judge-unravels-web-of-lies-20091125-jruq.html|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Bird free as judge unravels web of lies|first=Bellinda|last=Kontominas|date=26 November 2009}}</ref>

===Gold Coast Titans===
On 7 December 2009, Bird signed a one-year contract with the [[Gold Coast Titans]], starting in 2010, returning to the [[National Rugby League]] after being cleared of his glassing conviction.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/greg-bird-signs-with-titans/story-e6frexnr-1225807623614|title=Greg Bird signs with Titans|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=11 July 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/we-dont-want-greg-bird-broncos/news-story/e8ef37747a98a76e62c6dbe6c55fb93a|title=We don't want Greg Bird - Broncos|date=27 November 2009|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=11 July 2018}}</ref> In February 2010, he was voted in by the fans for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] team to play the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] in the inaugural [[All Stars Match]]. In May 2010, he was again selected for the [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|New South Wales Country Origin]] team in the annual City vs Country Origin match. In July 2010, he was selected for game 3 of the [[2010 State of Origin series]] for New South Wales. He scored a try. In November 2010, Bird played two matches for Australia in their [[2010 Four Nations]] campaign. After playing 22 games for the Titans in 2010, he played for the Titans in 2011 and 2012 while also returning to the representative arena.

In February 2011, Bird was again voted in by the fans for the Indigenous All Stars team to play the NRL All Stars in the annual All Stars Match. In 2011, he was selected for New South Wales in all three games of the [[2011 State of Origin series]].  Bird made a total of 19 appearances for the Gold Coast as they finished bottom of the table and claimed the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-defeat-the-gold-coast-titans-to-avoid-wooden-spoon-for-2011/news-story/fc87216a6a23a4b8d0eefd6174b60c47/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat the Gold Coast Titans to avoid wooden spoon for 2011|website=Daily Telegraph}}</ref>

In September 2011, he was again selected for the Prime Minister's XIII team to play Papua New Guinea. On 11 January 2013, he re-signed with the Titans on a four-year contract.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.titans.com.au/news-display/Bird-resigns-with-Titans-until-2017/67205 |title=Bird re-signs with Titans until 2017 - iSelect Gold Coast Titans |access-date=2013-01-11 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130203224045/http://www.titans.com.au/news-display/Bird-resigns-with-Titans-until-2017/67205 |archive-date=3 February 2013}}</ref> He was named a Titans co-captain for 2013 alongside [[Nate Myles]]. Bird was selected for Australia in the [[2013 ANZAC Test]] and played at second-row forward. In what was the first test match ever played in [[Canberra]], New Zealand were defeated.

In February 2012, Bird was again voted in by the fans for the Indigenous All Stars team to play the NRL All Stars in the annual All Stars match. In April 2012, he was again selected for the Country Origin team in the annual City vs Country Origin match. In 2012, he was again selected for New South Wales in all 3 games of the [[2012 State of Origin series]]. In 2013, Bird played in all three games of the [[2013 State of Origin series]] where NSW failed to win the shield once more.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/rugby-league-world-cup-2013-greg-bird-ready-for-hard-yards-20131108-2x6o2.html|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: Greg Bird ready for hard yards|first=Michael|last=Carayannis|date=8 November 2013|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref> In 2014, he played in Game 2 and 3 of the [[2014 State of Origin series]] where he was finally part of a winning series.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-ii-player-ratings-and-your-chance-to-have-your-say/news-story/e7f08d3e3ffba2b3bc8a675a37febf8a|title=Origin Interactive Player Ratings|date=18 June 2014}}</ref>
[[File:GREG BIRD (15753106725).jpg|thumb|right|Bird on international duty for the Kangaroos in 2014]]
On 8 December 2014, Bird was fined after he was caught urinating next to a police car in a carpark outside where he was celebrating his wedding. He told the media "It's put a dampener on our wedding weekend. I'm incredibly embarrassed and disappointed at myself."<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-12-08/greg-bird-allegedly-urinates-on-police-car-during-honeymoon/5951678|title=Greg Bird says public urination fine 'put dampener' on wedding weekend|website=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation]] |date=8 December 2014}}</ref>

In February 2015, Bird was stood down by The Gold Coast after he was issued a court notice along with other Gold Coast players for alleged cocaine supply.  On 7 October 2015, he had his charges thrown out of court.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2015-10-08/charges-against-titans-bird-and-harrison-dismissed/6836698|title=Bird's drugs charges dismissed but three former Titans to stand trial|website=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation]] |date=8 October 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/feb/22/drug-supply-scandal-gold-coast-titans-chaos|title=Greg Bird and Dave Taylor stood down by Gold Coast Titans amid drug scandal|agency=Australian Associated Press|date=22 February 2015|website=the Guardian}}</ref>

On 17 September 2016, Bird was alleged to have been involved in an incident at a Byron Bay pub and was placed under investigation by the NRL. On 18 October 2016, he was fined $550 after being found guilty of being intoxicated at the venue and refusing to leave after being instructed by security guards to do so.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/2016/10/18/11/11/greg-bird-fined-over-byron-bay-pub-antics|title=Greg Bird fined over Byron Bay pub antics - Nine Wide World of Sports|website=Wwos.nine.com.au |date=18 October 2016 }}</ref> At the end of 2016, he signed a five-year deal to join Super League side [[Catalans Dragons]].  Before leaving for France, Bird took one last swipe at The Gold Coast saying "I’m disappointed that like other people at the club I got pushed out the door."<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/titans/greg-bird-says-gold-coast-titans-pushed-him-out-the-door-as-his-turbulent-nrl-career-finishes/news-story/774ce48666f01b8d23cc1c7693efc009|title=NRL: Greg Bird swipes Gold Coast Titans for 'pushing' him to Super League|date=7 November 2016}}</ref>
